info.title: Key Service
info.description: Manages API keys and BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) credentials for organizations. Handles key generation, validation, encryption, and secure storage.

get.summary: Validate API key — returns user identity
get.description: Pass the API key to validate as ?key= query parameter. Authenticated via X-API-Key (service key).

get.summary: Get decrypted org key for a provider
get.description: Pass the user's API key as ?key= query parameter. Requires X-Caller-Service, X-Caller-Method, and X-Caller-Path headers to identify the calling endpoint. These are used to build the provider requirements registry.

schema.description: HTTP method of the caller's endpoint

parameters.description: HTTP method of the caller's endpoint
parameters.name: x-caller-method

400.description: Missing key parameter or required caller headers

get.summary: List user auth keys for an org

post.summary: Create a new user auth key
Get or create a default user auth key for the org+user

get.summary: List org keys for an org

post.summary: Add or update an org key
Add or update a platform key
post.description: Upsert a platform-level API key. Platform keys are global — not tied to any org. Typically registered once at deployment.

post.summary: Query which providers are needed for a set of endpoints
post.description: Given a list of service endpoints (service + method + path), returns which third-party providers each endpoint has been observed requesting. Used by workflow-service to determine which keys are needed before execution.

200.description: Provider requirements for the given endpoints

get.description: Returns the decrypted platform-level key for the given provider. No orgId/userId needed — platform keys are global. Requires X-Caller-* headers for provider requirements tracking.

schema.description: HTTP method of the caller's endpoint

parameters.description: HTTP method of the caller's endpoint
parameters.name: x-caller-method

get.description: Returns the decrypted key for a provider. Automatically resolves whether to use org or platform key based on the org's preference (default: platform). Response includes keySource indicating which was used. Requires X-Caller-* headers for provider requirements tracking. Always pass both orgId and userId — userId is required for logging.

schema.description: HTTP method of the caller's endpoint

parameters.description: HTTP method of the caller's endpoint
parameters.name: x-caller-method

400.description: Missing required parameters or caller headers

put.summary: Set key source preference for an org+provider
put.description: Set whether an org uses its own key ('org') or the platform key ('platform') for a given provider. If switching to 'org', an org key must already be stored — otherwise returns 400.

400.description: Invalid request or no org key stored

get.summary: Get key source preference for an org+provider
get.description: Returns the current key source preference for an org+provider. If no explicit preference is set, returns 'platform' with isDefault=true.

get.summary: List all key source preferences for an org
get.description: Returns all explicit key source preferences for an org. Providers not listed default to 'platform'.
